# Tunnels Protocol

## What it is
A purpose-built blockchain for recording contributions and distributing revenue deterministically. Not an EVM chain. Not a token. Infrastructure for tracking who did work and ensuring they get paid.

## Architecture
10 Rust crates, ~33,000 lines of code, 489 tests passing.
Full node binary (tunnels-node), CLI wallet (tunnels-wallet), WASM-compatible SDK (tunnels-sdk).
P2P networking via libp2p/gossipsub. State persistence via RocksDB. JSON-RPC API with 20+ endpoints.

## Core primitives
- Identity: non-transferable Ed25519 key pairs (Human or Agent type)
- Project: immutable container with distribution parameters set at creation
- Attestation: signed contribution record with economic bond, subject to challenge window
- Verification: time-windowed challenge period, binary outcome (finalized or rejected)
- Revenue Router: deterministic waterfall distribution with cascading remainders

## Key design decisions
- Single-key architecture: one key signs everything, prevents capital capture of reputation
- Append-only attestations: contribution history cannot be rewritten
- Deterministic distribution: same inputs always produce same outputs, no discretion
- Transparent parameters: all project economics visible at creation, immutable after
- Mandatory challenge windows: every attestation can be disputed before finalization

## Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/tunnelsprotocol/tunnels
- Whitepaper: https://tunnelsprotocol.org/tunnels-protocol-whitepaper.pdf
- Key Generator: https://tunnelsprotocol.github.io/tunnels/tunnels-keygen.html
